name: Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis
description: First Lady of the United States from 1961 to 1963
abstract: Jacqueline Lee Kennedy Onassis (née Bouvier; July 28, 1929 – May 19, 1994) was the first lady of the United States from 1961 to 1963, as the wife of John F. Kennedy, the 35th president of the United States.

about_quotes.speaker: Dorothy Donegan
about_quotes.evidence: I talked to Tommy Tucker and I said, “You getting married?” and he said, “I wouldn’t shack with Jackie Onassis.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Dorothy Donegan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: Hazel Scott, Mrs. Roosevelt, Jackie Onassis, Bobby Kennedy...you know, any of these people that you name, I've met.

about_quotes.speaker: Luther Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: when you turn Jackie Onassis away because you can't get a seat for her, you know you figure you must have arrived someplace
about_quotes.doc_title: Luther Henderson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: it probably goes back as far as Kennedy, simply because of Jackie Kennedy and her interest in the arts, period.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Bernhardt
about_quotes.evidence: when my mother got the invitation to come to the White House from Jackie Kennedy to hear me play
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Bernhardt interviewed by Monk Rowe, Bearsville, New York, January 3, 2012
